name: Phishing: Spearphishing Link
description: Adversaries send spearphishing emails containing malicious links to harvest credentials or deliver payloads. In Microsoft 365 environments, adversaries increasingly use AiTM (Adversary-in-the-Middle) phishing kits (Evilginx2, Caffeine, Tycoon 2FA) that reverse-proxy the real Microsoft login page to capture session cookies post-MFA. Defender for Office 365 Safe Links rewrites URLs and records click telemetry in UrlClickEvents. A successful AiTM campaign results in a valid session token replay logged in SigninLogs with a foreign IP but a successful MFA claim.
iocs: UrlClickEvents with ActionType 'ClickBlocked' or 'ClickAllowed' on URLs containing login-lookalike domains (login-microsoft[.]net, microsoftonline[.]xyz)
EmailEvents DeliveryAction 'Inbox' followed by UrlClickEvents click from same recipient within 5 minutes
SigninLogs ResultType 0 with AuthenticationRequirement 'multiFactorAuthentication' but IPAddress not matching the UrlClickEvents IP — AiTM token replay
UrlClickEvents IsClickedThrough == 1 on a URL that was later added to ThreatIntelligenceIndicator as phishing
Multiple recipients clicking the same URL within a short window — bulk phishing campaign
SigninLogs showing successful login from a Tor or datacenter IP immediately after a phishing click event in UrlClickEvents
OfficeActivity Operation 'MailItemsAccessed' from a new IPAddress within 10 minutes of successful sign-in following a phishing click
evasion_variants: Use a legitimate URL shortener or CDN redirect to obscure the final phishing domain from Safe Links analysis
Register the phishing domain with valid TLS certificate and age it 30+ days before use to pass domain-age checks
Deliver phishing link via Teams or SharePoint message rather than email to bypass email-centric detections
Use an open redirect on a trusted Microsoft or Office 365 domain (*.sharepoint.com, aka.ms) to redirect to adversary infrastructure
Implement a CAPTCHA gate on the phishing page to block automated Safe Links detonation crawlers
Time-limit the phishing link to expire after the first click to prevent retrospective detonation
Use a compromised legitimate website as the first-stage redirect to inherit the site's reputation score
detection_kql_hints: UrlClickEvents | where IsClickedThrough == 1 | join kind=inner (SigninLogs | where ResultType == 0) on $left.AccountUpn == $right.UserPrincipalName | where abs(datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, TimeGenerated)) < 30

SigninLogs | where ResultType == 0 and AuthenticationRequirement == 'multiFactorAuthentication' | where IPAddress has_any ('tor','anonymizer') or not(IPAddress matches regex @'^(10\.|172\.(1[6-9]|2[0-9]|3[01])\.|192\.168\.)')
EmailEvents | where UrlCount > 0 | join kind=inner (UrlClickEvents | where IsClickedThrough == 1) on NetworkMessageId | summarize count() by SenderFromDomain
UrlClickEvents | where Url matches regex @'(login|signin|microsoft|office365)[^/]*(\.(xyz|tk|ml|ga|cf|gq|live-[a-z]+\.com))' | summarize dcount(AccountUpn) by Url
Detect impossible travel: UrlClickEvents IPAddress geo vs SigninLogs IPAddress geo within same session — different countries within 10 minutes
